title: 10 best herbal tinctures for chronic hepatitis
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for chronic hepatitis are concentrated herbal extracts that can help manage this liver condition. They offer a natural alternative to conventional treatments and can be used to complement them. Some examples of herbal tinctures that may benefit individuals with chronic hepatitis include milk thistle, dandelion, and turmeric. Milk thistle, for instance, contains silymarin, which has been shown to protect the liver from damage and promote cell regeneration. Dandelion root can help improve liver function and reduce inflammation, while turmeric's active compound, curcumin, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect the liver from further damage. By incorporating these herbal tinctures into their treatment plan, patients with chronic hepatitis may experience improved liver function, reduced inflammation, and enhanced overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew tincture helps with chronic hepatitis because it contains parthenolide, a compound with potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties can help reduce liver inflammation, a key factor in hepatitis progression. Feverfew tincture may also help protect liver cells from damage caused by free radicals, which are produced in higher quantities during chronic hepatitis. Furthermore, feverfew has been shown to improve bile flow, supporting the liver's natural detoxification processes. By addressing these underlying factors, feverfew tincture can provide relief and potentially slow the progression of chronic hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Feverfew tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce liver inflammation caused by chronic hepatitis, thanks to the active constituents parthenolide and sesquiterpene lactones.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of feverfew tincture help protect liver cells from oxidative stress and damage, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Hepatoprotective: Feverfew tincture has hepatoprotective effects, which means it can help protect the liver from toxins and damage, partly due to the active constituent parthenolide.
Immune-modulating: Feverfew tincture can help regulate the immune response, reducing excessive inflammation in the liver, thanks to the active constituent parthenolide.
Analgesic: Feverfew tincture's analgesic properties help alleviate pain often associated with chronic hepatitis, due to the active constituents parthenolide and sesquiterpene lactones.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of feverfew are commonly used in medicinal teas for chronic hepatitis due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce liver inflammation.
Flowers: Feverfew flowers contain several active compounds, including parthenolide, that have been shown to protect the liver from oxidative stress and support overall liver health.
Seeds: Feverfew seeds are rich in essential oils and have been used in traditional medicine to support liver function and promote bile secretion, which can help improve digestion and detoxification.
remedy_recipe: Harvest feverfew leaves, ensuring they are fully grown and free from pesticides, then rinse and dry them.
Chop or crush 1 ounce of dried feverfew leaves and place them in a clean, dry glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of 80-100 proof vodka or rum over the crushed leaves, ensuring they are fully submerged, then secure the lid tightly.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.

Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out any excess liquid, and store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid. Take 1-2 mL up to three times a day for chronic hepatitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle tincture helps with chronic hepatitis because it contains silymarin, a potent antioxidant that supports liver health. This flavonoid complex helps to protect the liver from damage and promotes the regeneration of liver cells. Milk thistle tincture has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are common factors in chronic hepatitis. Furthermore, it inhibits the entrance of toxins into liver cells, preventing further damage. Regular consumption of milk thistle tincture may improve liver function, reduce symptoms, and enhance the overall quality of life for individuals with chronic hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Milk thistle tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce liver inflammation, a key factor in chronic hepatitis progression, due to the active constituent, silymarin.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant property of milk thistle tincture protects liver cells from oxidative stress and damage, which are common in chronic hepatitis, attributed mainly to silymarin and its components.

Hepatoprotective: Milk thistle tincture has a hepatoprotective property that supports liver cell regeneration and prevents toxin-induced damage, crucial for managing chronic hepatitis, owing to the active compound, silymarin.

Antifibrotic: The antifibrotic property of milk thistle tincture helps reduce liver fibrosis and scarring caused by chronic hepatitis, as silymarin inhibits the transformation of healthy liver cells into scar tissue.

Immunomodulatory: Milk thistle tincture's immunomodulatory property helps regulate the immune response, reducing immune-mediated liver damage in chronic hepatitis, due to the active constituent, silymarin.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of the milk thistle plant contain silymarin, a flavonoid that has been shown to have liver-protecting properties, making it useful for treating chronic hepatitis.
Leaves: The leaves of the milk thistle plant are also used in medicinal teas for chronic hepatitis, as they contain antioxidants that help protect the liver from damage.
Flowers: The flowers of the milk thistle plant are used in medicinal teas for chronic hepatitis due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the liver.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the milk thistle plant are used in medicinal teas for chronic hepatitis because they contain compounds that can help stimulate the production of bile, which is important for liver function.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried milk thistle seeds and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of vodka or rum with at least 40% alcohol content over the seeds until fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Store the final tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle and take 1-2 mL up to three times a day for chronic hepatitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tincture helps with chronic hepatitis because it contains curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compound. This compound can help reduce inflammation in the liver, a common symptom of hepatitis. Additionally, curcumin has been shown to improve liver function by increasing the production of bile, which aids in digestion and detoxification. Furthermore, its antioxidant properties can help protect the liver from damage caused by free radicals, which are produced in higher amounts in individuals with chronic hepatitis. Overall, turmeric tincture can help alleviate symptoms and improve liver health in individuals with chronic hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to its curcumin content, helps alleviate chronic hepatitis by reducing liver inflammation.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant capacity of turmeric tincture, attributed to curcumin, is important for chronic hepatitis as it combats oxidative stress and liver damage.

Hepatoprotective: Turmeric tincture's hepatoprotective effect, resulting from curcumin and other curcuminoids, is crucial for chronic hepatitis because it shields liver cells from toxins and injuries.
Antifibrotic: The antifibrotic property of turmeric tincture, attributed to curcumin, is significant for chronic hepatitis as it inhibits the formation of excessive scar tissue in the liver.
Immunomodulatory: Turmeric tincture's immunomodulatory effect, due to curcumin, is important for chronic hepatitis since it helps regulate the immune response, reducing liver inflammation and damage.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the turmeric plant are used to make medicinal tea for chronic hepatitis due to their high curcumin content, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help protect the liver.

Leaves: The leaves of the turmeric plant are used in medicinal tea for chronic hepatitis because they contain curcumin and other compounds that have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, helping to reduce liver damage and inflammation.

Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant are used in medicinal tea for chronic hepatitis due to their high curcumin content, which has been shown to have antifibrotic effects, helping to reduce liver fibrosis and improve liver function.

Flowers: The flowers of the turmeric plant are used in medicinal tea for chronic hepatitis because they contain curcumin and other compounds that have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, helping to reduce liver damage and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of fresh turmeric root into small pieces and place in a clean jar.
Add 2 cups of vodka or rum (80 proof) to the jar, ensuring the turmeric is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After 4-6 weeks, strain the liquid using a cheesecloth into a clean jar or bottle, squeezing out as much liquid as possible.
Discard the turmeric solids and store the tincture in a cool, dark place. Take 1-2 mL up to three times a day for chronic hepatitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common barberry tincture helps with chronic hepatitis because it contains berberine, an alkaloid that has been shown to have potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties make it effective in supporting liver health by reducing inflammation and fighting off harmful pathogens that can damage liver cells. Additionally, barberry has been shown to stimulate bile production, which aids in the digestion and elimination of toxins from the body. This is especially beneficial for individuals with chronic hepatitis, as the liver plays a critical role in filtering toxins and waste products from the blood. By supporting liver function and reducing inflammation, barberry tincture can help alleviate symptoms of chronic hepatitis and promote overall liver health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Barberry tincture can reduce inflammation in the liver, which is important for chronic hepatitis as it can help alleviate symptoms and potentially slow the progression of the disease. This property is due to the presence of berberine and other alkaloids.

Hepatoprotective: Barberry tincture has been shown to protect the liver from damage, which is crucial for chronic hepatitis as it can help prevent further liver damage and promote healing. The hepatoprotective effects are attributed to berberine and phellodendrine.

Antioxidant: Barberry tincture has antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress in the liver, important for chronic hepatitis as it can help protect liver cells from damage. The antioxidant activity is attributed to the presence of berberine, oxyberberine, and palmatine.

Antiviral: Barberry tincture has antiviral properties, which can help inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus, important for chronic hepatitis as it can help reduce the viral load and potentially slow the progression of the disease. The antiviral effects are attributed to berberine.

Immune-boosting: Barberry tincture can help boost the immune system, which is important for chronic hepatitis as it can help the body fight off the hepatitis virus. The immune-boosting effects are attributed to the presence of berberine and other alkaloids.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the common barberry plant contain berberine, an alkaloid that has been shown to have liver-protecting properties, making it useful for treating chronic hepatitis.

Bark: The bark of the barberry plant is rich in berberine and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress in chronic hepatitis.

Berries: The berries of the barberry plant contain high levels of vitamin C and other antioxidants that can help support liver function and reduce oxidative stress in individuals with chronic hepatitis.

Leaves: The leaves of the barberry plant contain berberine and other compounds that have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce liver inflammation and support liver function in chronic hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried barberry root bark into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of 100 proof vodka or grain alcohol over the herb, ensuring it is completely submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the tincture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solid material.
Store the finished tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Take 1-2 ml three times daily for chronic hepatitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Baical skullcap tincture helps with chronic hepatitis because it contains potent antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, particularly baicalin and wogonin, which are flavonoids found in the plant. These compounds have been shown to inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus and reduce inflammation in the liver. Additionally, baical skullcap has been found to protect liver cells from oxidative stress and damage, promoting overall liver health. Its hepatoprotective effects make it a valuable herbal remedy for individuals suffering from chronic hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Baical skullcap tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce liver inflammation, a key factor in chronic hepatitis, due to the presence of flavonoids like baicalein and wogonin.

Antioxidant: Its strong antioxidant properties protect liver cells from oxidative stress and damage, which are common in chronic hepatitis, thanks to the high concentration of phenolic acids and flavonoids.

Hepatoprotective: Baical skullcap tincture helps shield the liver from further damage caused by chronic hepatitis by promoting cell regeneration and preventing the depletion of glutathione, an essential antioxidant in the liver.
Antiviral: The tincture's antiviral properties inhibit hepatitis virus replication, slowing the progression of chronic hepatitis, owing to the presence of flavonoids and other active compounds.
Immunomodulatory: Baical skullcap tincture helps regulate the immune system, reducing immune-mediated liver damage in chronic hepatitis, by influencing cytokine production and T-cell function.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Baical skullcap tincture plant contain high levels of flavonoids, which have been shown to have liver-protecting properties, making them useful for treating chronic hepatitis.

Flowers: The flowers of the Baical skullcap tincture plant contain antioxidants that help to reduce inflammation and protect liver cells from damage, making them beneficial for managing chronic hepatitis.
Leaves: The leaves of the Baical skullcap tincture plant contain compounds that have been shown to have antiviral properties, which can help to slow down the progression of chronic hepatitis.

Seeds: The seeds of the Baical skullcap tincture plant contain essential oils and other compounds that have been shown to have a calming effect on the liver, making them useful for treating chronic hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried baical skullcap herb and place it in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or grain alcohol (80-100 proof) over the herb, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the excess liquid.
Store the tincture in a tightly sealed glass container in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Take 1-2 mL, 2-3 times daily, for chronic hepatitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tincture helps with chronic hepatitis because it has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-viral properties, which can reduce liver inflammation and inhibit the replication of the hepatitis virus. The active compound in licorice, glycyrrhizin, has been found to protect liver cells from damage and stimulate the production of interferon, a protein that plays a crucial role in the body's immune response to viral infections. Furthermore, licorice tincture can help to improve liver function by regulating the production of bile and promoting the repair of liver tissue. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before taking licorice tincture, as it can interact with certain medications and may not be suitable for individuals with certain health conditions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to the presence of glycyrrhizin, helps reduce liver inflammation in chronic hepatitis.
Hepatoprotective: The hepatoprotective effect of licorice tincture, attributed to its flavonoid content, shields liver cells from damage and supports liver function.
Antiviral: Licorice tincture's antiviral property, owing to the compound glycyrrhizic acid, inhibits hepatitis virus replication, slowing the progression of chronic hepatitis.

Immune-modulating: The immune-modulating effect of licorice tincture, stemming from its polysaccharide constituents, helps regulate the immune response, preventing further liver damage in chronic hepatitis.
Antioxidant: Licorice tincture's antioxidant property, provided by its phenolic compounds, combats oxidative stress, protecting liver cells from free radical damage in chronic hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice tincture plant contain glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-viral properties, making it useful for treating chronic hepatitis.
Flowers: The flowers of the licorice tincture plant contain flavonoids, which have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects and can help to reduce liver damage caused by chronic hepatitis.
Seeds: The seeds of the licorice tincture plant contain coumarins, which have been shown to have anti-fibrotic effects and can help to reduce liver fibrosis caused by chronic hepatitis.
Bark: The bark of the licorice tincture plant contains triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects and can help to reduce liver damage caused by chronic hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of dried licorice root into small pieces and place in a clean jar.
Pour 2 cups of 100-proof vodka or rum over the licorice root, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently once a day.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solids.
Store the finished licorice tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle, away from direct sunlight, for up to a year. Take 1-2 mL up to three times a day for chronic hepatitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tincture helps with chronic hepatitis because it promotes liver health. The herb contains potent antioxidants that protect the liver from damage caused by free radicals. Dandelion also stimulates the production of bile, which aids in digestion and the removal of toxins from the body. Its anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the liver, alleviating some of the symptoms of chronic hepatitis. Additionally, dandelion has been shown to improve liver function by increasing the flow of blood and oxygen to the liver. This can help support the liver's natural detoxification processes and promote overall liver health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion tincture's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones like taraxacin, can help alleviate liver inflammation in chronic hepatitis.

Hepatoprotective: The hepatoprotective effects of dandelion tincture, attributed to its antioxidant phenolic compounds, can help protect liver cells from damage and promote regeneration in chronic hepatitis.

Diuretic: Dandelion tincture's diuretic properties, resulting from its high potassium content and taraxacin, can help reduce fluid buildup in the body and ease the burden on the liver in chronic hepatitis.
Cholagogue: Dandelion tincture's cholagogue properties, due to its taraxacin content, can help stimulate bile production and flow, aiding in liver detoxification in chronic hepatitis.

Antiviral: Dandelion tincture's antiviral properties, attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones and phenolic acids, can help inhibit hepatitis virus replication and potentially slow the progression of chronic hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of dandelion are rich in therapeutic properties, making them a popular choice for treating chronic hepatitis due to their liver-protecting and detoxifying effects.
Leaves: Dandelion leaves are known for their ability to stimulate bile production and promote healthy liver function, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tea for chronic hepatitis.
Flowers: Packed with antioxidants, dandelion flowers help protect liver cells from damage, supporting overall liver health in those suffering from chronic hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dandelion plants, ensuring they have not been exposed to pesticides or pollutants, and rinse them thoroughly.
Chop or grind the dandelion roots, leaves, and flowers, then fill a clean jar tightly with the dandelion material, filling it up to three-quarters full.
Pour 80-proof vodka or rum into the jar, ensuring all plant material is submerged, and seal the jar tightly.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.

After 4-6 weeks, strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, and store the resulting dandelion tincture in a tightly sealed glass container in a cool, dark place. Recommended dosage is 1-2 mL, 2-3 times a day, for chronic hepatitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Katuki tincture helps with chronic hepatitis because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce liver inflammation and protect liver cells from damage. The active compounds in Katuki, such as picrosides and flavonoids, have been shown to stimulate the flow of bile, improve liver function, and promote the regeneration of liver tissue. Furthermore, Katuki tincture has been used in traditional medicine for centuries to treat various liver disorders, including hepatitis, jaundice, and liver cirrhosis. Studies suggest that Katuki tincture may help reduce liver enzyme levels, alanine aminotransferase (ALT), and aspartate aminotransferase (AST), which are indicators of liver damage in people with hepatitis. Therefore, Katuki tincture can be a valuable addition to a treatment plan for chronic hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Anti-hepatotoxic: Katuki tincture helps to protect the liver from damage and toxicity, which is important in chronic hepatitis as it can help to prevent further liver damage. This property is attributed to the active constituents such as iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and tannins.

Anti-inflammatory: Katuki tincture has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the liver, a key feature of chronic hepatitis. This property is due to the presence of iridoid glycosides and flavonoids.

Antioxidant: Katuki tincture has antioxidant properties that help to protect liver cells from oxidative stress, a common factor in chronic hepatitis. This property is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and tannins.

Hepatoprotective: Katuki tincture has hepatoprotective properties, which means it can help to protect and support liver function. This is important in chronic hepatitis as it can help to slow or stop the progression of the disease. The hepatoprotective effect is due to the presence of iridoid glycosides and tannins.

Immune-modulating: Katuki tincture has immune-modulating properties, which can help to regulate and balance the immune system. This is important in chronic hepatitis as an overactive or underactive immune system can contribute to the development and progression of the disease. This property is attributed to the active constituents such as iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and tannins.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help reduce liver inflammation and improve liver function in cases of chronic hepatitis.
Leaves: Rich in essential oils and flavonoids, which have been shown to protect the liver from damage and promote healing in cases of chronic hepatitis.
Flowers: Contain high levels of antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce liver inflammation and improve liver function in cases of chronic hepatitis.
Seeds: Rich in essential fatty acids and antioxidants, which can help reduce liver inflammation and improve liver function in cases of chronic hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried Katuki root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 16 ounces of vodka or rum (80-100 proof) over the chopped Katuki root, ensuring all pieces are submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid from the jar using cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, squeezing out all liquid from the Katuki root.
Store the final Katuki tincture in a cool, dark place and take 1-2 mL up to three times a day for chronic hepatitis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Andrographis tincture helps with chronic hepatitis because it contains potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can reduce liver inflammation and inhibit viral replication. The active compounds in andrographis, such as andrographolide, have been shown to protect liver cells from damage and promote liver cell regeneration. Furthermore, andrographis has been found to improve liver function by reducing elevated liver enzymes and decreasing oxidative stress in liver tissue. These therapeutic effects make andrographis tincture a valuable adjunctive treatment for individuals with chronic hepatitis, helping to alleviate symptoms and potentially slowing the progression of the disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Andrographis tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce liver inflammation, a key factor in chronic hepatitis, due to the presence of diterpene lactones like andrographolide.
Antiviral: Its antiviral property inhibits hepatitis virus replication, slowing down the progression of chronic hepatitis, attributed to the same diterpene lactones, particularly andrographolide.
Hepatoprotective: Andrographis tincture protects liver cells from damage, a common issue in chronic hepatitis, owing to key constituents like andrographolide and deoxyandrographolide.

Immunomodulatory: The herb's ability to regulate the immune system helps control immune responses in chronic hepatitis, which can exacerbate liver damage, due to its polysaccharides and diterpene lactones.

Antioxidant: Andrographis tincture's antioxidant property neutralizes free radicals, reducing oxidative stress in the liver, a contributing factor in chronic hepatitis, thanks to its phenolic acids and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of andrographis tincture plant are commonly used to make medicinal tea for chronic hepatitis due to their potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help reduce liver inflammation and improve liver function.

Leaves: The leaves of the andrographis tincture plant are used in medicinal tea for chronic hepatitis because they contain high levels of andrographolide, a compound that has been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, which can help protect the liver from damage and promote liver health.

Flowers: The flowers of the andrographis tincture plant are used in medicinal tea for chronic hepatitis due to their antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and protect liver cells from damage.

Seeds: The seeds of the andrographis tincture plant are used in medicinal tea for chronic hepatitis because they contain high levels of andrographolide and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and hepatoprotective effects, which can help reduce liver inflammation and improve liver function.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried Andrographis paniculata herb into small pieces and place in a clean jar.
Pour 5 ounces of 100-proof vodka or rum over the herb, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, squeezing out excess liquid from the herb.
Label the bottle with the name, dosage, and date, and store in a cool, dark place. Recommended dosage is 30-60 drops, 3 times a day.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat chronic hepatitis with herbal tinctures is by using a combination of milk thistle, dandelion root, and turmeric. Milk thistle aids in liver regeneration, dandelion root promotes bile production and turmeric has anti-inflammatory properties. Take these tinctures, as directed, consistently for best results. Always consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to chronic hepatitis that are treated with herbal tinctures include liver cirrhosis and non-alcoholic fatty liver disease. Milk thistle is a commonly used herb for treating these conditions, as it contains silymarin, an antioxidant that can help protect the liver from damage. Other herbs used in tinctures for liver ailments include dandelion root, turmeric, and artichoke. These herbs have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal treatments.
